channel.title: Tprrt's Blog - gamedev
channel.description: Embedded Linux, Zephyr RTOS, open-source hardware, Linux gaming, retro gaming, and competitive fitness

item.title: No Heap, No Problem: a Static Micro-ECS for the Game Boy Advance
item.description: How framer-engine's Entity Component System works on hardware that has no operating system, no virtual memory, and no malloc(), plus two real bugs the design caught before they shipped.
item.dc:creator: tperrot

item.title: Squeezing Cycles: Optimizing a Software 3D Renderer for the Game Boy
item.description: Real techniques, real cycle counts, and a couple of "obviously correct" optimizations that backfired, from porting a software 3D renderer to the Game Boy Advance's FPU-less ARM7TDMI in framer-engine.
item.dc:creator: tperrot

item.title: Open-Source Game Engines for Retro Consoles
item.description: A guide to modern open-source game engines and frameworks for developing games on classic consoles from 8-bit to sixth generation, all compatible with Linux.
item.dc:creator: tperrot
